title: dalea pinetorum
intro: Dalea pinetorum, also known as Pine Dalea, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Consuming Dalea pinetorum is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to make poultices and salves for topical applications.
